perturbation.original_text: In exchange for the Share Consideration, Licensor hereby grants to Licensee a non-exclusive, royalty-free, perpetual and non-perpetual license (subject to the duration and scope, and format limitations for which Licensor has the rights to each Title as specified in Schedule A1-A6 of Schedule A) to: i. license, exhibit, distribute, reproduce, transmit, perform, display, and otherwise exploit and make available each Title within the Territory in any language by VOD (including SVOD, TVOD, AVOD and free VOD) for Internet, TV and mobile platforms (including, but not limited to, OTT streaming services, Sites and Mobile Sites), subject to these limitations for each of the Title in Schedule A1-A6:
perturbation.changed_text: In exchange for the Share Consideration, Licensor hereby grants to Licensee an exclusive, fully-paid, perpetual license (subject to the duration and scope, and format limitations for which Licensor has the rights to each Title as specified in Schedule A1-A6 of Schedule A) to: i. license, exhibit, distribute, reproduce, transmit, perform, display, and otherwise exploit and make available each Title within the Territory in any language by VOD (including SVOD, TVOD, AVOD and free VOD) for Internet, TV and mobile platforms (including, but not limited to, OTT streaming services, Sites and Mobile Sites), subject to these limitations for each of the Title in Schedule A1-A6:
perturbation.explanation: The term 'non-exclusive' has been replaced with 'exclusive' and 'royalty-free' has been replaced with 'fully-paid'. This introduces a contradiction. Originally, the contract specified that the Licensee's right to the Titles was non-exclusive, meaning the Licensor could grant similar rights to other parties. Changing this to 'exclusive' means the Licensor cannot grant similar rights to anyone else. Also, originally the contract specified the license was royalty-free. Changing this to fully-paid, implies there should have been a payment to be made, thus creating a contradiction and leading to legal dispute and/or ambiguity
perturbation.contradicted_text: In exchange for the Share Consideration, Licensor hereby grants to Licensee an exclusive, fully-paid, perpetual license (subject to the duration and scope, and format limitations for which Licensor has the rights to each Title as specified in Schedule A1-A6 of Schedule A) to: i. license, exhibit, distribute, reproduce, transmit, perform, display, and otherwise exploit and make available each Title within the Territory in any language by VOD (including SVOD, TVOD, AVOD and free VOD) for Internet, TV and mobile platforms (including, but not limited to, OTT streaming services, Sites and Mobile Sites), subject to these limitations for each of the Title in Schedule A1-A6: 1) For Titles listed in Schedule A1-A2: Licensor can only grant Licensee distribution rights to up to six (6) MSOs plus two (2) of China's Internet TV license holders or their OTT Internet-based video partners by VOD (including SVOD, TVOD, AVOD and free VOD). China's current Internet TV license holders include: CNTV (中国网络电视台/未来电视), BesTV (百视通), Wasu (华数), Southern Media Cooperation (南方传媒), Hunan TV (芒果 TV), China National Radio/Galaxy Internet TV (GITV) (银河电视), and China Radio International (中国国际 广播电台); 2 Source: IDEANOMICS, INC., 10-K, 3/30/2016 2) For Titles listed in Schedule A3: For "天下女人": No satellite TV broadcasting rights granted. Titles other than "猜 猜女人心", "赏深越慕" , "职场新女性挑战行动", " 杨澜访谈录" (自第192期及以后的节 目),"人生相对论 ", " 天下女人", and "正青春" cannot be edited in any way; 3) For Titles listed in Schedule A4: Other than the versions (dubbed or translated) in which the Titles in Schedule A4 already exist in (and were delivered in), no further dubbing, translation, or editing may be performed by any party on Schedule A4's Titles. 4) For Titles listed in Schedule A5 (except "Summer in February"): Limitations on Rights Granted: the following distribution methods are permitted: non-theatrical, public video, ship and hotel rights (all not earlier than the US theatrical release); home video rental, home video sell through, and commercial video rights (all not earlier than the US video release); cable TV (pay & free), terrestrial TV (pay & free), satellite pay TV; internet TV (pay & free), residential and non-residential pay-per-view, residential and non-residential internet pay-per-view, VOD, near VOD, and internet VOD (all no earlier than the corresponding exploitation in the USA). Contents are dubbed and subtitled in Cantonese and Mandarin languages. Exploitation of the Title over the internet must adhere to the following: 1) Distribution is limited to the dubbed or subtitled version, provided the subtitles shall be burnt in with no option to remove them, 2) Licensor must be notified prior to the first exhibition of the Title over the internet, and 3) Authorization to use excerpts, stills, trailers and soundtrack parts of the Title for the 3 promotion of the Title only on Licensor's or Licensor's authorized agent's web site online. Source: IDEANOMICS, INC., 10-K, 3/30/2016 Rights granted for "Summer in February", shall adhere to Section 2, not subject to the limitations in section 2(a)(i)(4).
perturbation.justification: The original text grants a 'non-exclusive, royalty-free' license, while the changed text grants an 'exclusive, fully-paid' license. This directly contradicts the original agreement and creates a conflict with the contradicted text, which also grants an 'exclusive, fully-paid' license, but then lists the limitations. By changing from non-exclusive to exclusive and royalty-free to fully-paid, the changed text creates a direct contradiction regarding the nature and payment structure of the license, causing a structural flaw in the contract.

perturbation.original_text: Sections 2(a) [License Grant], 2(b) [Sublicensing], 2(c) [Display of Titles], 2(d) [Removal of Titles], 3, and 11 shall survive the expiration or termination of this Agreement: (i) in perpetuity with respect to Titles for which the licenses granted in Section 2(a) [License Grant] are perpetual; and (ii) for the duration of the applicable license term specified in Schedule A with respect to Titles for which the license term specified in Schedule A extends beyond the expiration or termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Sections 2(a) [License Grant], 2(b) [Sublicensing], 2(c) [Display of Titles], 2(d) [Removal of Titles], 3, and 11 shall not survive the expiration or termination of this Agreement. (i) in perpetuity with respect to Titles for which the licenses granted in Section 2(a) [License Grant] are perpetual; and (ii) for the duration of the applicable license term specified in Schedule A with respect to Titles for which the license term specified in Schedule A extends beyond the expiration or termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The phrase 'shall survive' was replaced with 'shall not survive' in Section 7(c). This directly contradicts the subsequent clauses within the same section that outline *how* those sections survive (i.e., in perpetuity or for a specified duration). If the sections do not survive at all, then the details of how they survive become meaningless, generating an uncertainty and contradiction. The clause now states that certain sections, including the crucial license grant, will *not* continue after the agreement ends, yet it also provides details on how long they *would* continue if they *did* survive, causing a disagreement with itself. Thus, a contradiction is created within Section 7, specifically 7(c).
perturbation.contradicted_text: Sections 2(a) [License Grant], 2(b) [Sublicensing], 2(c) [Display of Titles], 2(d) [Removal of Titles], 3, and 11 shall not survive the expiration or termination of this Agreement: (i) in perpetuity with respect to Titles for which the licenses granted in Section 2(a) [License Grant] are perpetual; and (ii) for the duration of the applicable license term specified in Schedule A with respect to Titles for which the license term specified in Schedule A extends beyond the expiration or termination of this Agreement. Sections 1, 7, 8(a) [Privacy and Data Collection; Reports], 12, 13, 14, 15, 16 and 17 shall survive any expiration or termination of this Agreement in perpetuity.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that certain sections 'shall not survive' the termination of the agreement, directly contradicting the subsequent clauses that specify how those sections *would* survive (in perpetuity or for a specified duration). This creates a clear in-text contradiction within the same section, as something cannot simultaneously not survive and have its survival duration defined. The contradicted text is the same changed text itself, because the first part of the sentence negates the second part, which causes a contradiction.
